insight: Stone Tablets represent the highest-authority knowledge artifact class in the LB substrate — above bedrock tablets because they carry a dual provenance chain: Miner-provenance (the corpus + IP-ledger chain) AND Founder-provenance (the keystone the Miner is anchored to). This double-anchor makes Stone Tablets the closest thing to 'the Founder said it AND we have all surrounding evidence.'
cj_implication: #2298 (Awareness Net) sub-claim — Stone Tablets are the highest-priority Eblet source because they carry Founder-authored-keystone provenance.

insight: K490 closes the Keystone-Compounding Loop: Founder articulates -> keystone registers -> inception event fires -> Miner spawns -> Stone Tablets accumulate -> future articulations encounter Stone Tablet substrate -> new articulations land denser. Each turn increases substrate density for the NEXT Founder articulation. This is a self-reinforcing knowledge architecture, not just a tagging scheme.
cj_implication: Standalone CJ candidate — the loop closure is novel and patentable as a knowledge-compounding mechanism.

insight: Phase A empirical: 62.7% of the 870,086 K487 real-corpus bedrock tablets match at least one Rhetorical Keystone (545,595 unique tablets tagged). This is substantially higher than K485's 43% on the Bishop memory corpus. The increase is partly explained by additional keystones added B120-B123 (#17-#29 = 13 new keystones), expanding coverage. The 62.7% rate means the LB corpus is deeply saturated with Founder-voice patterns — the substrate is already keystone-dense.
cj_implication: Wheelbarrow Empirical evidence: keystone-anchor rate scales with corpus breadth (43% bishop-only -> 62.7% full corpus), suggesting Keystones are not overfitted to bishop sessions.

cluster_label: KEYSTONE-19 as Top Stone Tablet Anchor
insight: KEYSTONE-19 ('Each Scribe a Voice. All as One.') produced the most Stone Tablets: 166,554 total. This is because 'all' and 'one' and 'voice' and 'scribe' are extremely common in the LB corpus — the 3-word epigraph touches almost every document about collective architecture, multi-agent systems, and cooperative structures. High-volume keystones like #19, #10 (medallions), and #06 (nothing about us) are semantically broad; future Sculptor work should weight by verbatim+paraphrase rather than raw count.
cj_implication: Stone Tablet confidence scoring (verbatim > paraphrase > thematic) is the correct downstream filter — thematic matches at 1.05M vs verbatim at 11K shows thematic is a starting signal, not a conclusion.

cluster_label: Verbatim Match Distribution Reveals Core Keystones
insight: Verbatim matches (11,060 total) are rare but high-signal. Top verbatim keystones: KEYSTONE-11 (7,200 verbatim — 'Help each other help ourselves' appears literally throughout Crown Letters), KEYSTONE-14 (1,400 verbatim — rising tide + wells), KEYSTONE-23 (550 verbatim — Ecclesiastes hand/might), KEYSTONE-08 (500 verbatim — people and leadership), KEYSTONE-09 (350 verbatim — No Plan Survives). These are the keystones that actually SURVIVED as verbatim phrases across the corpus — the strongest evidence of Founder-voice propagation.
cj_implication: Verbatim Stone Tablets should be surfaced preferentially in Sculptor distribution — they're evidence of literal Founder-phrase propagation across the corpus.

insight: All 30 Rhetorical Keystones produced at least some Stone Tablets. The lowest-count keystone is KEYSTONE-02 ('Especially from friendly fire.') with 100 tablets (all thematic), and KEYSTONE-04 ('And I have two suits.') with 450 tablets. These low counts are meaningful — they indicate the corpus rarely touches the Cardboard Boots register (military casualty / credibility-by-modesty) compared to cooperative-economics or cathedral-architecture topics. Zero-hit keystones = corpus coverage gap signal; low-hit keystones = rare-but-distinct Founder register.
cj_implication: Low stone tablet count per keystone is a substrate-coverage signal — future corpus ingestion should target materials relevant to low-anchor keystones (Crown Letters, Cardboard Boots letter specifically).

insight: K490 formalizes three Miner inception modes: 'root' (first Miner, bootstrapped), 'mitosis-spawned' (daughter from thematic divergence), 'keystone-spawned' (NEW — triggered by Founder articulation registered as Rhetorical Keystone). The keystone-spawned mode is the ONLY mode where Miner inception is triggered by a Founder speech-act rather than corpus structure. This means keystone-spawned Miners carry Founder-intentionality as their seed — they mine for evidence of what the Founder ALREADY SAID IS IMPORTANT, not what the corpus itself surfaces as topically significant.
cj_implication: #2296 (Miners) extension — keystone-spawned is a new inception pathway that crosses the Founder-speech-act layer with the corpus-mining layer.

cluster_label: File-Watch vs Explicit API — Robustness Architecture
insight: keystone_watcher.py implements Bishop's lean: explicit API (register_keystone()) as primary with file-watch polling as fallback. The file-watch polls keystones_registry.json (machine-readable JSON, K490-generated) rather than the human-authored project_rhetorical_keystones.md (Bishop memory). This separation ensures the watcher triggers on the VALIDATED registry (post-ratification) not on draft keystones in the memory file — matching the ratification model (Founder says it -> Bishop ratifies to memory -> Knight updates machine registry -> inception event fires).
cj_implication: Hybrid watch architecture (explicit API + file-watch fallback) is robust to both workflow patterns without requiring external dependencies.

cluster_label: Deduplication Key Design — Idempotency at Scale
insight: The dedup_key = tablet_id::keystone_id pattern ensures that re-running Phase A on the same corpus produces zero duplicate Stone Tablets. At 870,086 tablets × 30 keystones = 26.1M possible pairs, and 1,090,437 actual pairs tagged (~4.2% selection rate), the dedup set is manageable in memory (~180MB). For future runs on larger corpora, the dedup_log.json can be loaded once at start and used as a hash-set filter — O(1) per pair lookup, O(dedup_log) memory.
cj_implication: Idempotent retroactive tagging is a prerequisite for Stone Tablets being a reliable citation source — any system that generates duplicates is not trustworthy as an authoritative record.

insight: Stone Tablets stored in per-keystone JSONL files (30 files = KEYSTONE-00.jsonl through KEYSTONE-29.jsonl) enables efficient downstream filtering: a Sculptor serving KEYSTONE-11 (cooperative economics) only reads its file. Alternatively, a combined JSONL with keystone_anchor_id field enables cross-keystone queries (e.g. 'find all Stone Tablets matching EITHER #11 OR #00 OR #07'). Bishop's lean was per-keystone — K490 confirms this is correct for Sculptor use and performance.
cj_implication: CFP (#2292) compliance: each cathedral can subscribe to specific keystone streams rather than the full Stone Tablet set.

cluster_label: Biblical Resonance Architecture — Stone Tablets vs Bedrock
insight: The naming hierarchy is architecturally intentional: Bedrock (geological — foundation layer, permanent but mined) -> Stone Tablets (Biblical — most-authoritative-record, Mosaic, Ten-Commandments-class). Stone Tablets are a SUBCLASS of bedrock but carry the additional Founder-voice-anchor that makes them the highest-authority form. The 'Nodules of Knowledge' alternate name (geological) preserves the claim-language separation from 'Stone Tablets' (commercial-resonance name) — important for patent-filing where Biblical metaphor can attract prior-art challenges.
cj_implication: Two-name strategy: 'Stone Tablets' for public/commercial surfaces; 'Keystone-Anchored Bedrock Artifacts' or 'Nodules of Knowledge' in formal claim language.

cluster_label: Confidence Scoring as Downstream Trust Signal
insight: Three match tiers (verbatim/paraphrase/thematic) with distinct confidence ranges (0.90+/0.50-0.89/0.30-0.49) create a trust gradient that downstream consumers can filter on. The K487 distribution: 1.0% verbatim, 2.5% paraphrase, 96.5% thematic. This means the default Stone Tablet set is dominated by thematic (broad signal), and downstream consumers should use confidence >= 0.50 (verbatim + paraphrase only) for citation-grade use, and confidence >= 0.90 (verbatim only) for quote-grade use.
cj_implication: Confidence-filtered Stone Tablet subsets become the Seer's citation pool for the Awareness Net (#2298) — different query types draw from different confidence tiers.

cluster_label: K487 Corpus Breadth — 16,176 Files as Bedrock Substrate
insight: The K487 real-corpus run processed 16,176 files from the full LB workspace (letters, academic papers, innovation docs, prompt files, migration files, etc.) producing 867,909 tablets across 201 miners. Stone Tablet retroactive tagging touched 545,595 of these tablets (62.7%). The remaining 37.3% of tablets are corpus noise (technical/infrastructure content) with no Founder-voice signature — consistent with a code-heavy workspace where Founder-voice concentrates in letters and architecture docs.
cj_implication: The 37.3% untagged corpus is not evidence of keystone inadequacy — it's the technical infrastructure layer that doesn't need Founder-voice tagging. Stone Tablet coverage of Founder-authored content is likely near 100%.

cluster_label: Inception Audit Trail — Keystone-Compounding Loop Accountability
insight: keystone_inception_log.jsonl provides a complete audit trail of every inception event: which keystone, when, via which source (api/file-watch/retroactive). This audit trail is what makes the Keystone-Compounding Loop accountable — future Founder articulations can be traced back through (Stone Tablet -> keystone_anchor_id -> inception_event -> original Founder speech-act session). Closed provenance loop from Founder's words -> corpus evidence -> back to Founder context.
cj_implication: Inception audit trail is a prerequisite for the A&A #2281 claim 1(a) reduction-to-practice (heterogeneous AI client access with full provenance). Audit trail = provenance completeness.

cluster_label: Wheelbarrow at Scale: 62.7% vs 43% — Keystone Coverage Scaling
insight: K485 Wheelbarrow measured 43% Eblet-keystone-anchor-rate on the Bishop memory corpus (M-0001, ~2,177 tablets). K490 measures 62.7% on the full K487 real-corpus (870,086 tablets). The increase (43% -> 62.7%) is partly due to B120-B123 adding 13 new keystones (#17-#29), and partly because the real corpus contains proportionally more Founder-authored letters and architecture documents (dense with Founder voice) alongside the technical/infrastructure files. The M-0001 bishop-only rate measured in K490 is 63.7% — nearly identical to the real-corpus rate, confirming that Keystones generalize well beyond the bishop memory subset.
cj_implication: 62.7% keystone-anchor rate at 870k tablet scale is Wheelbarrow-grade empirical evidence for the Awareness Net (#2298) architecture — Founder voice is provably, measurably dense across the full LB corpus.

cluster_label: Stone Tablets as Sculptor Feed — Catacombs Integration Point
insight: Stone Tablets are designed to flow to Catacombs (#2258 Tower of Peace stack) as the highest-authority archive. The per-keystone JSONL files in stone_tablets/ are the natural input for Sculptor distribution (each Sculptor handles one keystone's Stone Tablet stream). The Sculptor reads its keystone's JSONL, filters by confidence, and distributes via CFP (#2292) to subscribing cathedrals. K490 establishes the full pipeline: Keystones -> Miners -> Stone Tablets -> Sculptors -> Catacombs -> Downstream surfaces.
cj_implication: K490 completes the Keystone-Compounding Loop implementation. Future sessions (K491+) can build the Sculptor distribution layer on top of the Stone Tablet files.

cluster_label: KEYSTONE-19 Surprise — Epigraph as Corpus Saturation Signal
insight: KEYSTONE-19 ('Each Scribe a Voice. All as One.') being the top Stone Tablet anchor with 166,554 tablets is a SURPRISE — it was ratified only in B121 (very recently). Its high count reflects that 'all', 'one', 'voice', 'scribe' are ubiquitous in the LB corpus vocabulary — not specifically because this epigraph is constantly quoted. This reveals a measurement challenge: broad keystones produce thematic-match noise. The fix: weight Stone Tablet significance by (verbatim_count + 0.5*paraphrase_count) rather than raw total. KEYSTONE-19 verbatim=0, paraphrase=12 — truly novel/rare in the corpus.
cj_implication: Stone Tablet significance scoring is the next optimization target — raw count inflated by thematic sensitivity should not drive Sculptor priority. Verbatim+paraphrase weighted score is the correct signal.
